topic: Meta-prediction: Will CP on Dems House control be above 9%
summary: CP exactly at 9.00% threshold with only 22 forecasters. Status quo = NO. Low forecaster count creates high variance. Underlying event is unlikely given 220-212 R majority.
content:
Key analysis:
1. Current CP: 9.00% (exactly at threshold, NOT above it)
2. Threshold: strictly above 9.00% to resolve YES
3. Status quo (no change) = NO
4. Only 22 forecasters = high variance, single forecaster can move median
5. Resolution check: 2026-01-14 13:30:06 (point-in-time check, ~11 days away)
6. Underlying event extremely unlikely: Republicans have 220-212 majority, Democrats would need a massive swing
7. No strong catalysts expected in 11 days to dramatically shift opinion
8. The meta-question launch could attract new forecasters to underlying question
9. Recency-weighted median means recent forecasts matter more

Factors favoring NO (~55%):
- Status quo resolves NO
- Underlying event is very unlikely, which may push forecasts down
- Republicans have strengthened majority through 2025
- No CP history available but 9% already accounts for long-tail risks

Factors favoring YES (~45%):
- Only 22 forecasters = very volatile
- One new forecaster at 12-15% could push median above 9%
- Meta-question launch may attract attention and new forecasters
- Random noise could easily move CP by 1-2pp in either direction
- Any news about Republican departures could briefly spike estimates
